phases.name: The Ash Locket
phases.description: CAMPBELL has flagged a physical anchor object — a small gold locket associated with the Keller University entity. Warm to the touch at all ambient temperatures. Lab directive: priority acquisition. Locket is believed to be in the possession of the entity's charge.
phases.lab_note: Acquire and transport intact. No field analysis until locket is secured. Handle with standard anomalous-object protocols.

phases.name: The Ash Locket
phases.description: A small gold locket, origin Hungary. Contained an unclassified grey particulate (Class 2 Ectoplasmic Residue, per CAMPBELL). Warm to the touch at all ambient temperatures. Left a frost ring on any surface it rested on. Inside: a handwritten note in Hungarian. Translation on file. The locket was not retrieved — the entity dispersed at scene. Trace ash recovered incidentally by field team and submitted for analysis.
phases.lab_note: Residue exhibits anomalous interaction with active electronic systems. Preliminary findings suggest the particulate acts as a conductive medium for signals outside the standard electromagnetic spectrum. Research continues. Implications for Project Veil: under assessment.

phases.name: — PENDING FIELD RECOVERY —
phases.description: CAMPBELL has flagged a probable anchor object associated with the Aldermoor District anomaly. Origin of disturbance traced to a construction site excavation 18 months prior. Physical object, if recovered, would constitute first confirmed spatial-anchoring artefact. Lab has requested priority acquisition. Field team advised: object may be unstable outside affected zone.

phases.name: Diane Marsh's Field Notebook
phases.description: Nine days of observations recorded from inside the Aldermoor displacement zone by district nurse Diane Marsh, 58. Charted intensity cycles, navigation windows, street-by-street behaviour. Contains a hand-drawn sketch of a threshold-shaped shadow in the excavation substrate — described as "a door-shaped shadow in the rock — not a door, but the memory of one." Drawn at dawn, three days before extraction. Object of interest to multiple researchers. Significance of threshold sketch: under assessment.
phases.lab_note: Notebook constitutes the most detailed first-person account of spatial consciousness displacement recovered to date. Threshold sketch cross-referenced against known liminal boundary maps. Flagged for operative Alan Frazier's review — possible resonance with Changeling geographic sensitivity. Research continues.

phases.name: — PENDING —
phases.description:
NO FURTHER ARTEFACTS LOGGED.
LOG WILL UPDATE POST-SESSION.
